grade_rationale: All 4 HIGH findings from first run resolved. Structural integrity solid, headless mode well-specified, state scan scripted, open-floor opening present. Remaining issues are enhancements (headless config scalar, resume table, context carry verification) and script hygiene (tests, PEP 723). Upgraded from B- to B+.
resolved_from_first_run: O4: resume protocol — PARTIALLY RESOLVED (singular timestamp)
O8: phân tích trigger — RESOLVED (specific compound phrases)

findings.title: No unit tests for scan-phase1-state.py
findings.description: Script runs on every activation. No scripts/tests/ directory exists. Regressions in glob matching, frontmatter parsing, or next_recommended logic would silently degrade greeting status.
findings.fix: Create scripts/tests/test-scan-phase1-state.py with cases: dir not found, all missing, partial, all present, frontmatter date extraction, next_recommended ordering.

findings.title: Missing PEP 723 inline dependency block
findings.description: No # /// script block. str | None syntax requires Python 3.10+ but no version constraint declared. uv run compatibility blocked.

findings.title: Headless mode has no customize.toml configuration scalar
findings.description: Teams wanting always-headless must inject -H in every pipeline invocation. No 'configure once' option.
findings.fix: Add headless_default = false to customize.toml. In SKILL.md headless section: 'Also activates if {agent.headless_default} is true.'

findings.title: Resume protocol surfaces only most recently updated artifact
findings.description: When multiple artifacts exist with different ages, only the most recent timestamp shows. User doesn't see that D-02 may be stale.
findings.fix: Surface compact status table with all timestamps. Scan script already returns updated for all artifacts.

findings.title: Context carry depends on downstream skill behavior without verification
findings.description: 'Mention the predecessor path so the workflow can load it' — effectiveness depends on receiving skill actually loading mentioned path. If it doesn't, context carry silently fails.
findings.fix: Either verify downstream skills load mentioned paths, or promote to active distillation at dispatch (read key terms/REQ IDs from predecessor and pass as context capsule).

findings.title: Headless scope of config loading is ambiguous
findings.description: Headless says 'Execute agent block resolution, config loading, and scan' but headless only needs output_path — full config (user_name, communication_language) is unnecessary.
findings.fix: Specify: 'Execute only agent block resolution (for output_path) and scan. Skip config.yaml and persona loading.'

findings.title: {document_output_language} loaded but never used
findings.description: Embody Persona loads document_output_language from config but agent produces no documents — dispatches to sub-skills that produce them.

findings.title: Scan script has no meaningful exit codes
findings.description: status: blocked exits 0, indistinguishable from complete to shell callers.
findings.fix: Add sys.exit(1) when blocked, sys.exit(2) on caught exceptions.

findings.title: Scan fallback references unresolved {agent.output_path}
findings.description: If resolver fails, fallback says 'check {agent.output_path} manually' — but that token is also unresolved. Need hardcoded default.

findings.title: D-02/D-03/D-06 codes appear before menu decodes them
findings.description: Status shows artifact codes before menu maps them to readable names. Minor friction for first-timers.
findings.fix: Expand status to include artifact names: 'D-02 Requirements: missing' instead of 'D-02: missing'.

findings.title: No soft-gate elicitation at workflow transitions
findings.description: After workflow completes, returns to menu without 'anything else about [artifact] before we move on?' moment.
findings.fix: Add: 'After artifact confirmation, briefly ask if there's anything to adjust before presenting menu.'

findings.title: Overview domain framing slightly redundant
findings.description: Two sentences say the same thing about cost of vague requirements compounding downstream.

strengths: All 4 first-run HIGH findings resolved — output_path, open-floor, headless, resume all implemented
Headless mode well-specified with precise JSON schema and testable status contract
State scan scripted with scan-phase1-state.py — deterministic, supports -o for file output
Script fallbacks present for both resolver and scan (both branches independently executable)
Open-floor opening correctly implemented with 3-branch routing (direct dispatch / absorb context / invite)
Menu dispatch carries predecessor paths at dispatch time
customize.toml shape correct — key-merge on menu items, scalars documented
Decision log records genuine trade-offs with quality analysis entries
